steps.title: Identify zeros from the factored form
steps.content: The function $f(x) = (x+6)(x+5)(x-4)$ equals zero when any factor equals zero: at $x = -6$, $x = -5$, or $x = 4$. These are exactly the three $x$-values in the table.

steps.title: Evaluate y = f(x) - 3 at the zeros
steps.content:
At each zero of $f(x)$, the function value is 0. So for $y = f(x) - 3$:
  • $x = -6$: $y = 0 - 3 = -3$
  • $x = -5$: $y = 0 - 3 = -3$
  • $x = 4$: $y = 0 - 3 = -3$
All three outputs equal $-3$. Only choice B has all $y$-values equal to $-3$ — B is correct.
